status_notes: Initial research from Google BigQuery documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference and live environment validation.
notes: Serverless data warehouse. Security includes encryption, IAM, column/row-level security, data masking, VPC Service Controls.

match_nodes.description: Encryption at rest with Google-managed or customer-managed keys

data.description: All data in BigQuery is encrypted at rest. Google-managed keys by default. Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) available via Cloud KMS at the dataset or table level.

options.name: Google-Managed Keys
options.description: Default. Google manages encryption keys. No additional configuration.

options.name: Customer-Managed Keys (CMEK)
options.description: Customer manages keys in Cloud KMS. Set at dataset level (default for new tables) or per-table.

default.note: All data encrypted at rest with Google-managed keys by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use CMEK for regulatory compliance. Configure at dataset level to apply to all new tables.

audit.note: Check defaultEncryptionConfiguration in the dataset response.

match_nodes.description: IAM at project, dataset, table, and view level

data.description: IAM access control at project, dataset, table, and view level. Fine-grained predefined roles: BigQuery Data Viewer, Data Editor, Data Owner, Job User, Admin. Dataset-level ACLs control who can query data.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use dataset-level IAM for most access control. Avoid project-level BigQuery Admin grants. Use authorized views for cross-dataset access.

audit.note: Check the access list in the dataset response.

match_nodes.description: Views that access data across datasets without granting underlying table access

data.description: Authorized views grant access to query results without granting access to the underlying tables. The view's SQL query determines what data is exposed. Useful for sharing data across datasets or projects while restricting access to raw data.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use authorized views to share data across teams without exposing underlying tables. Combine with row/column-level security for fine-grained control.

match_nodes.description: Policy tags restrict access to specific columns

data.description: Column-level security uses Data Catalog policy tags to restrict access to specific columns. Requires creating a taxonomy in Data Catalog, creating policy tags, applying them to columns, and granting Fine-Grained Reader role.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use column-level security for columns containing PII or sensitive data. Combine with data masking for graduated access.

audit.note: Check policyTags field in the column schema.

configure.note: Create taxonomy in Data Catalog, create policy tags, apply to columns via schema update, grant roles/datacatalog.categoryFineGrainedReader to authorized users.

match_nodes.description: Row access policies filter which rows a user can see

data.description: Row access policies filter rows returned by queries based on the querying user's identity. Created via SQL CREATE ROW ACCESS POLICY statement. Multiple policies on a table are OR-ed together.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use row-level security for multi-tenant data and when different users should see different subsets of rows.

configure.sql: CREATE ROW ACCESS POLICY {policy_name} ON {project}.{dataset}.{table} GRANT TO ('user:{email}', 'group:{group}') FILTER USING ({predicate});
configure.note: Users with bigquery.admin or bigquery.rowAccessPolicies.overrideTimeTravelRestrictions bypass RLS. TRUE filter function grants access to all rows.

match_nodes.description: Dynamic data masking with policy tags

data.description: Dynamic data masking applied through policy tags in Data Catalog. Users without Fine-Grained Reader role see masked values instead of actual data. Multiple masking rules available.

options.name: SHA-256 Hash
options.description: Replace with SHA-256 hash of the value.

options.name: Default Masking Value
options.description: Replace with a default value for the data type (empty string, 0, etc.).

data.vendor_recommendation: Use data masking with column-level security to provide graduated access. Requires BigQuery Enterprise tier.

audit.console: Data Catalog -> Taxonomies -> {taxonomy} -> {policy tag} -> Data masking rule

match_nodes.description: Restrict BigQuery API access to a service perimeter

data.description: VPC Service Controls restrict BigQuery API access to a defined service perimeter. Prevents data exfiltration by blocking queries and data transfers from outside the perimeter, even with valid credentials.
data.vendor_recommendation: Configure VPC Service Controls for BigQuery in production environments containing sensitive data.
